---
title: "GP Modeling"
jupyter: python3
---

```{=html}
<style>
/* pandas/quarto dataframe tables inherit Bootstrap's .table (width:100%),
   which stretches them across the full page-layout container; size to
   content instead. */
table.dataframe { width: auto; max-width: 90%; font-size: 0.85em; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto; }
</style>
```

```{python}
#| label: py-helpers
#| include: false

DEPTH_LABEL = {"ohc_700": "0–700 m", "ohc_2000": "0–2000 m"}
TABSET_FIGW, TABSET_FIGH = 17, 5

def _emit(md):
    print("\n" + md + "\n")

def _show_fig(fig):
    _emit('<div style="text-align:center">')
    display(fig)
    _emit("</div>")
    plt.close(fig)
```

# Setup

This report picks up where `nac.qmd`'s Analysis Region / OHC Anomaly sections leave off, without
re-running any of that report's plotting or re-deriving its climatology. `nac.qmd` publishes the
ANALYSIS_BOUNDS-restricted, multi-year truth field and the truth-vs-climatology anomaly cells to
`data/ohc_truth/`; loading those directly (~1s) skips both the expensive depth-integration step
(cached separately, per year, as `data/ohc_truth/truth_ohc_<year>.nc`) and the RG climatology
reprocessing, neither of which this report needs to repeat.

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| code-summary: "Setup — imports and experiment configuration"
import sys, os
sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath("../code"))

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import xarray as xr
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

import ohc
import ohc_bias
import ohc_climatology as ohc_clim

YEARS = [2020, 2021, 2022]

ANALYSIS_BOUNDS = (36, 40, -68, -62)
EXTENT_WIDE     = [-76, -47, 28, 50]
OHC_TRUTH_DIR   = "../data/ohc_truth"
DEPTHS          = ["ohc_700", "ohc_2000"]
```

```{python}
#| label: load-cached-data
#| code-summary: "Load nac.qmd's published truth field and truth-vs-climatology anomaly"
truth_field_all = xr.open_dataset(f"{OHC_TRUTH_DIR}/truth_field_analysis.nc").load()
truth_anom_cells_all = pd.read_csv(f"{OHC_TRUTH_DIR}/truth_anom_cells_all.csv", parse_dates=["month"])
```

```{python}
#| label: load-real-argo
#| include: false
argo_all = pd.read_csv("../data/argo_ohc.csv")
argo_all["date"] = pd.to_datetime(argo_all["date"], format="ISO8601")
```

# Real Argo Float Trajectories

Real Argo floats that profiled the analysis domain at any point during 2020-2022, from
`data/argo_ohc.csv`.

```{python}
#| label: fig-real-argo-traj
#| fig-cap: "Real Argo floats, 2020-2022"
from trajplots import map_point_trajectories

la0, la1, lo0, lo1 = ANALYSIS_BOUNDS
ids = argo_all.loc[
    argo_all.lat.between(la0, la1)
    & argo_all.lon.between(lo0, lo1)
    & argo_all["date"].dt.year.isin(YEARS),
    "float_id",
].unique()
argo_a = argo_all[argo_all.float_id.isin(ids)]
print(f"{len(ids)} floats entered the analysis domain during {YEARS[0]}-{YEARS[-1]}")

_ = map_point_trajectories(
    argo_a,
    extent=EXTENT_WIDE,
    boxes=[(*ANALYSIS_BOUNDS, "analysis domain")],
    title=f"All obs for floats that profiled the domain, {YEARS[0]}-{YEARS[-1]}",
)

argo_a = argo_a[
    argo_a["date"].dt.year.isin(YEARS)
    & argo_a.lat.between(la0, la1)
    & argo_a.lon.between(lo0, lo1)
]

_ = map_point_trajectories(
    argo_a,
    extent=EXTENT_WIDE,
    boxes=[(*ANALYSIS_BOUNDS, "analysis domain")],
    title=f"{YEARS[0]}-{YEARS[-1]} obs within the analysis domain",
)
print(f"{len(argo_a)} profiles within the analysis domain, {YEARS[0]}-{YEARS[-1]}")
ohc_bias.monthly_float_counts(argo_a).rename(
    columns={
        "n_floats": "n_floats_real",
        "n_profiles": "n_profiles_real",
    }
)
```

# GP Interpolation with Real Floats

## Background

We interpolate real Argo float OHC (`ohc_700`/`ohc_2000` directly, not their anomaly relative to
climatology) onto the full analysis-domain grid at the native 1/12$^\circ$ resolution, using a Gaussian
process fit with `GpGp`. Each real float profile is assigned the **GLORYS truth OHC**  at its position and date.

`code/ohc_gp_fit.R` fits one GP per depth (0-700m, 0-2000m) with the stationary spatiotemporal
covariance function `matern_spheretime`, pooling all profiles across all three years, with
covariance parameters $\theta$ = ($\sigma^2$, $\rho_s$, $\rho_t$, $\nu$, $\tau^2$) = (variance,
spatial range, temporal range, smoothness, nugget). Smoothness is fixed at $\nu = \tfrac{1}{2}$,
where the Matérn form reduces to the simple exponential $k(r) = e^{-r}$.

The temporal coordinate is a continuous day count (days since 2020-01-01), not day-of-year -- so
the fitted temporal range can extend across years rather than being capped at within-year scale by
an annual wraparound, letting the model express long-range temporal autocorrelation if the data
support it.

Fitting and prediction are separate scripts, so one fit is reused across both prediction grids
below. `code/ohc_gp_predict.R` predicts via a custom Vecchia kriging step (`kriging_predict`) that
gives both a mean and a standard error -- `GpGp::predictions()` alone is faster but has no SE.
Predicting on the coarse 1° grid (35 points) conditions each point on every one of the ~3,450
pooled profiles ("exact" mode) -- cheap at that grid size, and matches the reference. Predicting on
the native 1/12° grid (~3,600 points x 36 months x 2 depths) with that same full conditioning does
not scale -- so that pass instead uses a bounded Vecchia neighbourhood (`m = 30`, matching the
fit's own approximation), which reuses the same underlying machinery but conditions each point on
only its 30 nearest profiles.

## Model Fit

```{python}
#| label: gp-real-prep
#| code-summary: "Sample GLORYS truth OHC at each real Argo profile's position/date"
_dates = xr.DataArray(pd.to_datetime(argo_a["date"]).values, dims="profile")
_lats  = xr.DataArray(argo_a["lat"].values, dims="profile")
_lons  = xr.DataArray(argo_a["lon"].values, dims="profile")
_truth_at_real = truth_field_all.interp(time=_dates, latitude=_lats, longitude=_lons, method="nearest")

real_argo_direct = argo_a[["lat", "lon", "date"]].copy()
for _d in DEPTHS:
    real_argo_direct[_d] = _truth_at_real[_d].values
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-gp-fit-diagnostics
#| code-summary: 'Fit the pooled GP once, then predict on the 1$^\circ$ (exact) and native 1/12$^\circ$ (bounded Vecchia) grids'
_GP_DIR = "../data/gp_interp"
os.makedirs(_GP_DIR, exist_ok=True)
_real_profiles_csv    = f"{_GP_DIR}/nac_gp_profiles_real_direct.csv"
_real_fit_summary_csv = f"{_GP_DIR}/nac_gp_real_direct_fit_summary.csv"
_real_model_cache     = f"{_GP_DIR}/nac_gp_real_direct_model_cache.rds"

_keys = ["month", "cell_lat", "cell_lon"]
_GP_MONTH_DAY = "middle"
_GP_RES_LABELS = {1 / 12: "1/12$^\circ$ (native)", 1.0: "1$^\circ$"}
# Full conditioning ("exact") is fine at 35 points; the ~3,600-point native
# grid needs the bounded Vecchia approximation to stay fast -- see Background.
_GP_PREDICT_M = {1 / 12: 30, 1.0: "exact"}
_pred_grid_csvs = {deg: f"{_GP_DIR}/nac_gp_pred_grid_{deg:.4g}deg.csv" for deg in [1 / 12, 1.0]}
_real_out_csvs  = {deg: f"{_GP_DIR}/nac_gp_real_predictions_{deg:.4g}deg.csv" for deg in [1 / 12, 1.0]}

def _grid_lats_lons(deg):
    """Prediction grid for one resolution -- native truth grid, or a deg-degree coarsening of it."""
    if deg < 0.1:
        return truth_field_all["latitude"].values, truth_field_all["longitude"].values
    _tc = ohc.coarsen_truth(truth_field_all, deg=deg)
    return np.sort(_tc["cell_lat"].unique()), np.sort(_tc["cell_lon"].unique())

for deg, grid_csv in _pred_grid_csvs.items():
    lats, lons = _grid_lats_lons(deg)
    ohc_clim.build_pred_grid(lats, lons).to_csv(grid_csv, index=False)

ohc_clim.write_profile_csv(real_argo_direct, _real_profiles_csv, suffix="")
# Fit + predict are the expensive steps (pooled GP fit, bounded-Vecchia predict
# on the ~3,600-point native grid); skip both if the fit summary and model
# cache are already on disk. Delete both files to force a re-fit/re-predict.
if not (os.path.exists(_real_fit_summary_csv) and os.path.exists(_real_model_cache)):
    ohc_clim.run_gp_fit(_real_profiles_csv, _real_fit_summary_csv, _real_model_cache)
    for deg, out_csv in _real_out_csvs.items():
        ohc_clim.run_gp_predict(
            _real_profiles_csv, _pred_grid_csvs[deg], _real_model_cache, out_csv,
            m=_GP_PREDICT_M[deg], month_day=_GP_MONTH_DAY,
        )

gp_fit_summary = ohc_clim.load_gp_fit_summary(_real_fit_summary_csv)
gp_fit_summary.groupby("depth")[["loglik", "converged", "n_obs"]].first()
```

```{python}
#| label: gp-interp-prep
#| code-summary: "Load the GP-predicted OHC field directly (no climatology to add back)"
def build_gp_res_ctx(out_csv, deg, profiles_df=None):
    """Load GP predictions from out_csv and assemble the display context for resolution deg.

    Modeling OHC directly (suffix="" throughout) means the GP's prediction *is*
    the field -- no per-year climatology reassembly needed here, unlike the
    anomaly-modeling case.
    """
    lats, lons = _grid_lats_lons(deg)
    gp_field = ohc_clim.load_gp_anomaly_field(out_csv, lats, lons, suffix="")

    cells    = ohc_clim.to_cells_table(gp_field)
    se_cells = ohc_clim.to_cells_table(gp_field, value_cols=("ohc_700_se", "ohc_2000_se"))
    obs_cells = (
        ohc.grid_cells(profiles_df, list(DEPTHS), deg=1 / 12)
        if profiles_df is not None else None
    )

    if deg < 0.1:
        truth_monthly_all = truth_field_all.resample(time="1MS").mean().rename({"time": "month"})
        _bf = (gp_field[list(DEPTHS)] - truth_monthly_all[list(DEPTHS)]).rename(
            {d: f"{d}_bias" for d in DEPTHS}
        )
        bias_cells  = ohc_clim.to_cells_table(_bf, value_cols=[f"{d}_bias" for d in DEPTHS])
        truth_cells = ohc_clim.to_cells_table(truth_monthly_all)
    else:
        # coarsen_truth resamples+bins by each row's own date, so one call over
        # all three concatenated years already yields distinct per-year cells.
        truth_by_deg_all = ohc.coarsen_truth(truth_field_all, deg=deg)
        _merged = cells.merge(
            truth_by_deg_all[_keys + list(DEPTHS)].rename(columns={d: f"{d}_truth" for d in DEPTHS}),
            on=_keys, how="left",
        )
        bias_cells = _merged.copy()
        for _d in DEPTHS:
            bias_cells[f"{_d}_bias"] = bias_cells[_d] - bias_cells[f"{_d}_truth"]
        truth_cells = truth_by_deg_all

    return dict(
        cells=cells, se_cells=se_cells, obs_cells=obs_cells,
        bias_cells=bias_cells, truth_cells=truth_cells,
        obs_profiles=profiles_df,
    )

def build_gp_ctx(out_csvs, profiles_df=None):
    return {_GP_RES_LABELS[deg]: build_gp_res_ctx(out_csv, deg, profiles_df) for deg, out_csv in out_csvs.items()}

_real_ctx = build_gp_ctx(_real_out_csvs, profiles_df=real_argo_direct)
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-gp-fit-summary
#| code-summary: "Format the pooled GP fit parameter table for display"
def _fmt_fit_summary(df):
    d = df.copy()
    d["display"] = d.apply(
        lambda r: f"{r['estimate']:.4g}" if pd.isna(r["std_error"])
        else f"{r['estimate']:.4g} +/- {r['std_error']:.3g}",
        axis=1,
    )
    wide = d.pivot(index="parameter", columns="depth", values="display")
    order = ["mean (intercept)", "variance", "spatial_range", "temporal_range", "smoothness", "nugget"]
    return wide.reindex([p for p in order if p in wide.index])

EARTH_RADIUS_KM = 6371.0
_scale = {
    "mean (intercept)": ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ, "variance": ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ ** 2,
    "spatial_range": EARTH_RADIUS_KM, "temporal_range": 1.0,
    "smoothness": 1.0, "nugget": 1.0,
}
_disp = gp_fit_summary.copy()
_disp["estimate"] = _disp.apply(lambda r: r["estimate"] * _scale[r["parameter"]], axis=1)
_disp["std_error"] = _disp.apply(lambda r: r["std_error"] * _scale[r["parameter"]], axis=1)
fit_table = _fmt_fit_summary(_disp)
fit_table.index.name = "parameter"
fit_table
```

**Units** mean (GJ m$^{{-2}}$), variance (GJ m$^{{-4}}$), spatial_range (km), temporal_range (days), nugget (relative fraction of variance)

## Predictions

```{python}
#| label: gp-color-scales
#| include: false
# Shared absolute-OHC colour range across every map below.
_truth_monthly_all_cells = ohc_clim.to_cells_table(
    truth_field_all.resample(time="1MS").mean().rename({"time": "month"})
)
VLIM = {
    d: (float((_truth_monthly_all_cells[d] * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ).quantile(0.02)),
        float((_truth_monthly_all_cells[d] * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ).quantile(0.98)))
    for d in DEPTHS
}
```

```{python}
#| label: gp-interp-maps
#| output: asis
#| code-summary: "GP-interpolated field vs native-grid truth, its standard error, and the GP - truth bias map"
_CI_BAND_LEVELS = (50, 75, 90, 95, 99)
_CI_BAND_LABELS = (
    [f"within {_CI_BAND_LEVELS[0]}% CI"]
    + [f"{lo}-{hi}% CI" for lo, hi in zip(_CI_BAND_LEVELS[:-1], _CI_BAND_LEVELS[1:])]
    + [f"beyond {_CI_BAND_LEVELS[-1]}% CI"]
)

def make_gp_map(c, kind, d, res_label):
    if kind == "obs":
        _lats, _lons = _grid_lats_lons(1 / 12)
        return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(
            c["obs_cells"], d, vmin=VLIM[d][0], vmax=VLIM[d][1],
            scatter_dots=True, width=TABSET_FIGW, ncols=12,
            lats=_lats, lons=_lons,
            title=f"Observed Argo {d} by month ({res_label})")
    if kind == "gp":
        return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(
            c["cells"], d, vmin=VLIM[d][0], vmax=VLIM[d][1], width=TABSET_FIGW, ncols=12,
            title=f"GP-interpolated {d} by month ({res_label})")
    if kind == "gp_se":
        return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(
            c["se_cells"], f"{d}_se", vmin=0, cmap="viridis", width=TABSET_FIGW, ncols=12,
            cbar_label=f"{d} GP standard error (GJ m$^{{-2}}$)",
            title=f"GP standard error, {d} ({res_label})")
    if kind == "truth_native":
        return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(
            c["truth_cells"], d, vmin=VLIM[d][0], vmax=VLIM[d][1], width=TABSET_FIGW, ncols=12,
            title=f"GLORYS truth {d} by month ({res_label})")
    if kind == "gp_bias":
        bcol = f"{d}_bias"
        bmax = float(c["bias_cells"][bcol].abs().max()) * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ
        return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(
            c["bias_cells"], bcol, vmin=-bmax, vmax=bmax, cmap="RdBu_r",
            cbar_label=f"{d} bias (GJ m$^{{-2}}$)", width=TABSET_FIGW, ncols=12,
            title=f"GP - truth bias, {d} ({res_label})")
    if kind == "gp_bias_se":
        z_cells = ohc_bias.compute_standardized_bias_cells(c["bias_cells"], c["se_cells"], f"{d}_bias", f"{d}_se")
        zmax = float(np.nanmax(np.abs(z_cells["z"])))
        return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(
            z_cells, "z", vmin=-zmax, vmax=zmax, cmap="RdBu_r", value_scale=1,
            cbar_label="(GP - truth) / SE", width=TABSET_FIGW, ncols=12,
            title=f"GP - truth bias in SE units, {d} ({res_label})")
    if kind == "gp_coverage":
        band_cells = ohc_bias.compute_bias_ci_band_cells(
            c["bias_cells"], c["se_cells"], f"{d}_bias", f"{d}_se", levels=_CI_BAND_LEVELS
        )
        return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(
            band_cells, "band", vmin=0, vmax=len(_CI_BAND_LEVELS), discrete=True,
            cmap="RdYlGn_r", value_scale=1, tick_labels=_CI_BAND_LABELS,
            width=TABSET_FIGW, ncols=12, cbar_label="predicted-truth gap, by nested CI band",
            title=f"Prediction-interval band, {d} ({res_label})")

GP_MAP_KINDS = [("obs", "Observed OHC"),
                ("obs_table", "Observed OHC table"),
                ("gp", "Predicted OHC"),
                ("gp_se", "Prediction Standard Error"),
                ("truth_native", "True OHC"),
                ("gp_bias", "Prediction Error"),
                ("gp_bias_se", "Prediction Error (standardized)"),
                ("gp_coverage", "Prediction Interval Bands")]

def make_gp_table(c, d):
    profiles = c.get("obs_profiles")
    if profiles is None:
        return None
    month = pd.to_datetime(profiles["date"]).dt.to_period("M").astype(str)
    tbl = profiles.groupby(month)[d].agg(["count", "mean", "std", "min", "max"])
    tbl.loc["Year"] = profiles[d].agg(["count", "mean", "std", "min", "max"])
    tbl.index.name = "month"
    tbl = tbl.rename(columns={"count": "n", "std": "sd"})
    scale_cols = ["mean", "sd", "min", "max"]
    tbl[scale_cols] = (tbl[scale_cols] * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ).round(3)
    tbl["n"] = tbl["n"].astype(int)
    return tbl

def emit_gp_map_tabset(ctx):
    _emit("::: {.panel-tabset}")
    for d in DEPTHS:
        _emit(f"## {DEPTH_LABEL[d]}")
        _emit("::: {.panel-tabset}")
        for res_label, c in ctx.items():
            _emit(f"### {res_label}")
            _emit("::: {.panel-tabset}")
            for kind, label in GP_MAP_KINDS:
                _emit(f"#### {label}")
                if kind == "obs_table":
                    tbl = make_gp_table(c, d)
                    if tbl is not None:
                        display(tbl)
                else:
                    _show_fig(make_gp_map(c, kind, d, res_label))
            _emit(":::")
        _emit(":::")
    _emit(":::")

emit_gp_map_tabset(_real_ctx)
```

## Coverage Rates

For each grid cell and month, we check whether the truth OHC falls within the GP's predicted
value +/- a chosen multiple of its kriging standard error, at two widths: +/-1 SE and the wider
+/-1.96 SE (`scipy.stats.norm.ppf(0.975)`), i.e. a nominal two-sided 95% prediction interval.
Summarizing either indicator over all cells in a month gives a **coverage rate**: if the kriging SE
is well calibrated and interpolation errors are approximately Gaussian, each rate should hover near
its interval's nominal Gaussian coverage (68.3%, 95%). A rate consistently *below* its reference
line means that interval is too narrow (the GP is overconfident at that width); consistently
*above* means it is too wide (overly conservative).

```{python}
#| label: gp-coverage-rate
#| output: asis
#| fig-cap: "Monthly +/-1 SE and 95% CI coverage rate vs the nominal Gaussian rate"

_COVERAGE_INTERVALS = {"+/-1 SE": 1.0, "95% CI": ohc_bias.Z_95}
_COVERAGE_LINESTYLES = {"+/-1 SE": "--", "95% CI": "-"}
_COVERAGE_NOMINAL = {lbl: ohc_bias.nominal_coverage(z) for lbl, z in _COVERAGE_INTERVALS.items()}

def emit_gp_coverage_tabset(ctx):
    _emit("::: {.panel-tabset}")
    for res_label, c in ctx.items():
        _emit(f"### {res_label}")
        cov_by_depth = {}
        for d in DEPTHS:
            cov_by_depth[DEPTH_LABEL[d]] = {
                lbl: ohc_bias.monthly_coverage_rate(
                    ohc_bias.compute_coverage_cells(c["bias_cells"], c["se_cells"], f"{d}_bias", f"{d}_se", z=z)
                )
                for lbl, z in _COVERAGE_INTERVALS.items()
            }
        _show_fig(ohc_bias.plot_coverage_by_month(
            cov_by_depth, nominal=_COVERAGE_NOMINAL, linestyles=_COVERAGE_LINESTYLES,
            title=f"Monthly coverage rate ({res_label})"))
        display(ohc_bias.coverage_rate_table(cov_by_depth))
    _emit(":::")

emit_gp_coverage_tabset(_real_ctx)
```
